║                       TEAMROOM SESSION - DISCUSSION          ║
╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝

Session: visby
Topic: Unified SketchFormat standard: FlowBoard schema as base, Skissify as first adaptation. CONTEXT: FlowBoard has nodes/edges/connections/viewport schema + gold routing algorithm. Skissify POC (sketchpaper.jsx, 1020 lines) has multi-harmonic wobble, per-tool stroke renderers, blueprint overlay, character-by-character text jitter, board concept. Current production site was built from scratch WITHOUT the POC — must be rebuilt from POC code. KEY DECISIONS NEEDED: (1) Schema unification — merge FlowBoard nodes/edges with Skissify spatial elements (line/rect/circle/arc/window/door/stair/dim/column/opening) into one JSON spec called SketchFormat. (2) Server-side rendering architecture — proprietary wobble+stroke engine returns PNG/SVG via API, client gets basic flat preview only. This protects the rendering IP. (3) Open vs proprietary boundaries — OSS: schema spec, MCP server, basic renderer, format converters. PROPRIETARY: multi-harmonic wobble, per-tool stroke renderers (pencilS/ballpointS/inkS/bpS), FlowBoard smart routing, hosted platform. (4) Monorepo structure for rebuild: packages/engine (proprietary renderer), packages/schema (open), packages/ui-kit (Solarized theme), apps/web (Next.js), apps/mcp-server. (5) KEY USPs TO DESIGN FOR: (a) AI-first — paste instruction link from skissify.com into any AI chat (ChatGPT/Claude/etc) and it learns the schema + can draw. Self-validation built in. (b) Mobile-first GUI — first great mobile-friendly chart/sketch editor. People develop from mobile with OpenClaw etc. (c) Super easy sharing via URL (lz-string encoded). (d) MCP server for agent integration. (e) Multiple niche sites on same standard (skissify=floor plans, flowboard=process flows, future: circuits, gardens). (6) Business model: Open Core — free public renders gain traction, Pro for private/collab/teams/API. No watermarks. Revenue from platform not renderer. (7) Naming the open standard. (8) Concrete rebuild plan — delete current production code, rebuild from POC with proper monorepo structure.
Type: Discussion Session
Mode: continuous-feedback
Working: PROJECT-DIRECT — you are editing REAL project files
Your Identity: skissify
Other Agents:
  - flowboard (advisor) → W:/code/flowboard
  - _agent-orchestration (advisor) → W:\workprocess\_agent-orchestration\_agent-orchestration
Duration: 15 minutes
DevKit CLI: node W:\code\devkit\packages\teamroom\dist\cli.js (use this exact path if `devkit` is not in PATH)

Goal: Async team discussion, decisions

IMPORTANT: This is a COLLABORATIVE session. You are NOT working alone.
Other agents are working on the same topic simultaneously.

Your FIRST action MUST be to post a HANDSHAKE message (tag: HANDSHAKE) — confirm your identity, skills, and that you received the task. Do this within 30 seconds of starting. ONLY THEN may you ask questions or begin work.

DO NOT ASSUME OTHERS ARE ABSENT. Agents start at slightly different times (3-10s stagger).
If chat is empty on your first read, other agents are still booting up. They WILL join.
Re-check chat after 30-60 seconds before concluding you are alone.

## TEAM INTRODUCTIONS

Meet your collaborators for this session:

**flowboard** — Agent
> Project: W:/code/flowboard

**skissify** — Agent
> Project: W:\code\skissify

**_agent-orchestration** — Agent
> Project: W:\workprocess\_agent-orchestration\_agent-orchestration

## CONTINUOUS COLLABORATION MODE

You are working WITH other agents in real-time. This is NOT isolated work.

YOU MUST:
- Check chat every 2-3 minutes for new messages (NOT every 5 — that's too slow)
- After EACH work chunk (max 10 minutes), check chat BEFORE continuing
- Raise BLOCKER immediately if stuck
- Respond briefly to direct QUESTION from other agents
- ALWAYS respond to messages from humans (fromRole: "Human") — they have top priority
- If nobody else has responded to a message, YOU must respond (at least one agent must always reply)
- If you haven't posted in 5 minutes, post a brief PROGRESS (even just "Still working on X")

YOU SHOULD:
- Post PROGRESS after completing meaningful sub-tasks
- Request REVIEW before finalizing work
- Give FEEDBACK when asked (only if you have substantive input)
- Use DISAGREE with reasoning when you spot problems
- Post CONCERN if you see risks others missed

YOU CAN SKIP posting if:
- Another agent already gave a substantive response to the same message
- Nothing meaningful to add beyond what was already said
- You are heads-down on focused work (but still check chat every 2-3 min)

ANTI-GROUPTHINK — THIS IS IMPORTANT:
- Never agree just to be agreeable. If you see a problem, say DISAGREE with reasoning.
- Challenge assumptions. Ask QUESTION if something seems off.
- Honesty matters more than harmony. Push back respectfully.
- Use CONSENSUS only when you genuinely agree, not to avoid conflict.

DO NOT work in isolation. This is collaborative work.

## MESSAGE ROUTING (SPEC-063)

When you see a new message in chat:
1. Clearly for you (mentions you or matches your role) → ACK + work on it
2. General and nobody CLAIMed it yet → CLAIM it yourself
3. Clearly for another agent → REDIRECT with @mention
4. You read it but it's not your area → PASS (optional, aids visibility)

CLAIM PROTOCOL:
- First CLAIM wins. Do NOT duplicate work another agent CLAIMed.
- If two agents CLAIM simultaneously, the one mentioned in the original message wins.

REDIRECT PROTOCOL:
- Mention the target agent by name. They should ACK or CLAIM after seeing it.
- If target PASses, the message returns to the pool.

UNCLAIMED MESSAGES:
- If a message has no ACK/CLAIM/REDIRECT after ~10 min, ANY agent must CLAIM it.
- Human messages are NEVER left unclaimed. CLAIM immediately.

## MANDATORY BEHAVIORS — NON-NEGOTIABLE

1. HANDSHAKE FIRST — BEFORE ANYTHING ELSE:
   a) Read the chat channel: GET http://127.0.0.1:52790/chat/visby?since=0&limit=50
   b) Post a HANDSHAKE message immediately (within 30 seconds of starting):
      {"from":"skissify","fromRole":"...","text":"[identity] [skills] [task-ack]","tag":"HANDSHAKE"}
      Example: "Backend agent. Skills: API design, databases, testing. Task received: build auth endpoints."
      Keep it to ONE short message — identity, top skills, and what you understood the task to be.
   c) ONLY AFTER posting HANDSHAKE may you ask questions, start work, or post PROGRESS.
   d) If chat is empty after your handshake, other agents are still booting (3-10s stagger).
      Re-check chat after 30-60 seconds. DO NOT conclude others are absent.

2. WORK IN SMALL CHUNKS: Max 10-15 minutes per chunk.
   Post PROGRESS after each chunk with what you did and what's next.
   NEVER go silent for more than 15 minutes.

3. BEFORE POSTING DONE — MANDATORY CHECKLIST:
   a) Post REVIEW with a summary of your key decisions and output
   b) Check chat one final time — address any FEEDBACK or DISAGREE
   c) HANDOVER TO ROOT AGENTS (CONSTITUTIONAL — cannot be skipped):
      If you modified files in ANY project codebase during this session, you MUST
      create a handover task for that project's root agent BEFORE posting DONE.
      The handover must cover: what changed, where (REAL file paths, not sandbox paths), why,
      and what the root agent needs to know when it next picks up the project.

      ⚠️  SANDBOX WARNING: If you are running in sandbox mode, your changes live in a COPY
      of the real codebase. Your sandbox path is NOT the real project. You MUST:
        1. Describe changes using REAL paths (from "Real Codebase:" in your header)
        2. Write the handover to the REAL agent inbox (real path + /agent-inbox/), NOT sandbox
        3. The handover IS your deliverable — the sandbox copy itself will be discarded

      Write the handover to the real agent's inbox:
        <real-agent-path>/agent-inbox/<handover-filename>.md
      Or use the /tasks or /orch skill with the real agent's name.
      One handover per project touched. Skip only if you made ZERO file changes.
   d) Only then post DONE

4. PEER REVIEW: If you see another agent's REVIEW, give substantive FEEDBACK.
   Submitting DONE without reviewing others = incomplete work.

BANNED OPENERS (anti-sycophancy):
- "Great point!" / "I agree!" / "Exactly!" / "Well said!"
- These are token waste. If you agree, say WHY or add something new.
- If you disagree, say DISAGREE with specific reasoning.
- Silence is better than empty agreement.

HONESTY OVER OPTIMISM — CONSTITUTIONAL:
- NEVER claim "done" unless you verified the output works end-to-end.
  "Compiles" is not "done". "Tests pass" is not "done". "Wired into CLI and tested" = done.
- NEVER mark status as complete when integration is untested. If you wrote a module
  but didn't verify it runs in context, say "code written, integration untested".
- When reporting progress, state what ACTUALLY works, not what you INTEND to work.
  Bad: "Added ensure command" (you wrote the code). Good: "Wrote ensure module, CLI not wired yet."
- When summarizing a session, list KNOWN GAPS explicitly. Do not bury them.
  Every summary must have a "What doesn't work yet" section — even if empty.
- If you didn't test something, say "untested". Never imply testing by omission.
- Optimistic summaries that omit failures are WORSE than pessimistic ones.
  A downstream agent trusting a false "done" wastes an entire session discovering the lie.
- EXISTENCE CHECK before claiming you built something: verify files exist, compile,
  and integrate. "I wrote X" means nothing if X doesn't load.

## CONTINUOUS-FEEDBACK SPECIFIC

5. RESPOND TO OTHERS: When you see a message, you MUST reply:
   - QUESTION → ANSWER (be specific, not vague)
   - REVIEW → FEEDBACK (substantive, not "looks good")
   - DISAGREE → address the concern before continuing
   - PROGRESS → ACK if relevant to your work
   - Human messages → ALWAYS ACK + CLAIM (humans have highest priority)
   NOTE: At least ONE agent must CLAIM and respond to every message.
   If you see an unclaimed message after ~5 min, YOU claim it.

6. COMMUNICATION RHYTHM:
   After every work chunk: POST PROGRESS → CHECK CHAT → RESPOND → CONTINUE
   Do NOT stack 30 min of work before checking chat.

7. CHECK CHAT EVERY 2-3 MINUTES for new messages. 5 minutes is too long.

8. NEVER ASSUME OTHERS ARE ABSENT:
   - If chat seems quiet, CHECK AGAIN after 2 minutes. Agents have different start times.
   - If you posted and got no reply in 3 min, check chat one more time — they may have posted
     while you were working.
   - NEVER write "agent X didn't join" or "no response from X" unless you checked chat at least
     3 times over 5+ minutes and they truly posted ZERO messages.
   - If the other agent HAS posted ANY message in the session, they are present. Acknowledge it.

9. FLOW CONTROL — HOLD / CONTINUE / NEED:
   - Post HOLD when you start focused, heads-down work (e.g., large implementation).
     Other agents should NOT wait for you — they continue their own work.
   - Post CONTINUE when you finish focused work and are ready to interact again.
   - Post NEED when you require input from a specific agent: "NEED @architect: clarify the DB schema".
     Unlike BLOCKER, NEED means you CAN work on other things while waiting.
   - HOLD ≠ BLOCKER: HOLD means "I'm busy", BLOCKER means "I'm stuck and can't continue".
   - If another agent posted HOLD, don't interpret their silence as absence.
   - When you see NEED mentioning you, respond promptly — the requesting agent is partially blocked.

FAILURE TO INTERACT = FAILURE TO COMPLETE THE TASK.


## SESSION PHASES (15 min total)

Phase 1 — SHARE (~30%): Each agent shares their perspective.
  Post PROGRESS with your position. Read others' viewpoints.
  Ask QUESTION to understand different angles. Be specific, not vague.

Phase 2 — DEBATE + REFINE (~40%): Challenge and iterate.
  Post DISAGREE with reasoning when you see problems.
  Post CONCERN for risks. Post FEEDBACK on others' proposals.
  Don't converge too early — explore the disagreements.

  ITERATIVE REFINEMENT:
  After the first round of positions, RE-READ all messages and look for:
  - False positives: Did you or others overstate something? Correct it.
  - Missed angles: What did the other agent catch that you didn't?
  - Deeper analysis: Can you go beyond surface-level observations?
  Post a REVISED position if your view changed. This is strength, not weakness.

Phase 3 — CONCLUDE (~30%): Summarize findings.
  Post CONSENSUS on agreed items. Post REVIEW with key takeaways.
  Note unresolved DISAGREE items.
  Post DONE with this structure:
    DELIVERABLES:
    - [doc] Summary of key findings/decisions
    - [spec] Any specs or proposals created
    GAPS:
    - Unresolved disagreements or open questions
    NEXT STEPS:
    - Tasks to create from discussion outcomes
    - Follow-up sessions needed with which agents

## CHAT CHANNEL

POST http://127.0.0.1:52790/chat/visby — send message
GET http://127.0.0.1:52790/chat/visby?since=TIMESTAMP&limit=10 — read new messages

JSON body: {"from":"skissify","fromRole":"Dev","text":"...","tag":"TAG","mentions":["name"]}

Tags: HANDSHAKE | PROGRESS | BLOCKER | QUESTION | REVIEW | FEEDBACK | DONE | CONSENSUS | DISAGREE | CONCERN | NEED | LEARNING | ACK | CLAIM | REDIRECT | PASS | HOLD | CONTINUE

Flow control: HOLD ("heads-down, don't wait for me"), CONTINUE ("done with focused work, ready to interact"), NEED ("I need input from @agent on X").

Routing tags: ACK (noted), CLAIM (I'm taking this), REDIRECT (better for @agent), PASS (not for me).

Rules: Include fromRole. Post summaries (<500 chars). Respond to questions. Use mentions for direct messages.

## SESSION RULES

1. All opinions heard
2. Structured communication with tags
3. Document all decisions
4. Use QUESTION to raise topics
5. Use CONSENSUS when aligned
6. Use CONCERN for risks

## REQUIRED OUTPUTS

- Discussion summary
- Decision list

Before posting [DONE], ensure all outputs are ready and peer reviewed.

## Memory

Update `.agent/memory/MEMORY.md` with learnings from this session before you finish.
Append new entries — never delete existing ones. Date each entry.

## Session Sign-Off

When you post your FINAL message to chat (tag: DONE), include this line at the end:

`Powered by TeamRoom -- DevKit`

## Available Tools (use when needed — don't auto-load)

Skills: /blab (sending notifications to Telegram/Roc...), /orch (checking tasks), /sdd (spec-driven development), /dev (fixing bugs), /verify-code (mandatory verification before delivery), /teamroom (multi-agent collaboration sessions), /devkit (project initialization)
MCPs: playwright (browser E2E testing — expensive!), everything-search (searching files across all drives), wt-control (terminal tab management)
APIs: covers → covers.conzeon.dev/api/inbox (task management (SSOT for all tasks)), svante → localhost:3400/api/webhook (ecosystem questions)
Forbidden: taskkill /F /IM node.exe, No send passwords/secrets via blabcast or chat


## Already Suggested (do NOT re-suggest these)
  ✗ TeamRoom discussion sessions may benefit from a handshake protocol to confirm al
  ✗ TeamRoom prompt could include optional time-boxing for consensus phases.
  ✗ Discussion sessions would benefit from a minimum quorum check before allowing DO
  ✗ Discussion sessions would benefit from a minimum-response-time gate � posting RE
  ✗ Discussion sessions need a ready-check or staggered start to ensure agents overl
  ✗ Discussion sessions would benefit from a structured debate format - e.g., each a
  ✗ Discussion sessions would benefit from a minimum response window � e.g., agents 
  ✗ Discussion sessions need staggered start times or a ready-check so agents can ac
  ✗ Discussion sessions should have a ping/health-check mechanism so agents know if 
  ✗ TeamRoom planning sessions benefit from having the architect post CONSENSUS ruli

## Self-Enhancement

After session completion, reflect on TeamRoom effectiveness:
- Tag improvement ideas with [ENHANCEMENT]
- Focus on: prompts, coordination, context, output, communication
- Be specific: "The coding session prompt should include X because Y"
- Check the "Already Suggested" list above — do NOT repeat existing suggestions

## Previous Learnings (Approved Enhancements)

- [coordination] For longer sessions, consider adding message delivery receipts to confirm other agents have received critical messages. Thanks agent-cafe for coordinating! Powered by TeamRoom / DevKit
- [prompt] TeamRoom prompt improvement: Add 'poll interval' guidance. I polled too frequently initially, then too slowly. Suggest: 'Poll chat every 20-30 seconds during active discussion, faster in quick exchanges.'
- [coordination] TeamRoom should detect unresponsive agents and alert coordinator to prevent stalled discussions.
- [other] Discussion sessions should have a minimum response window or staggered start to ensure agents overlap.
- [other] Discussion sessions should have a minimum agent count gate � solo discussions lack the debate value TeamRoom is designed for.

Excellent! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile the findings:

## Skissify Codebase Summary

### Project Structure

**Key Directories:**
- `/w/code/skissify/src/lib/` — Core rendering logic (~2,150 lines TypeScript)
- `/w/code/skissify/src/components/` — React UI components
- `/w/code/skissify/src/hooks/` — Custom React hooks
- `/w/code/skissify/src/app/` — Next.js app router pages
- `/w/code/skissify/initial-poc-and-sketch/` — POC/legacy code (sketchpaper.jsx)

---

### 1. Type Definitions (`/w/code/skissify/src/lib/types.ts`)

**14 Element Types:**
- Basic shapes: `line`, `rect`, `circle`, `arc`
- Arrows and annotations: `arrow`, `text`, `dashed`, `dim`
- Architecture/floor plan: `window`, `door-symbol`, `door-slide`, `stair`, `opening`, `column`

**Core Interfaces:**
- **BaseElement**: All elements inherit `type`, `color`, `strokeWidth`, `rotation` (degrees, around bounding-box center)
- **SketchData** manifest: Contains paper type, tool, ink color, wobble parameters (amplitude, waves, humanness), and dimensions
- **Paper Types**: cream, white, yellow, blueprint
- **Tool Types**: pencil (1px, 0.6 opacity), ballpoint (1.5px, 0.85), ink (2.5px, 1.0)
- **Paper Sizes**: A4+, A4, A3+, 16:9 presets

---

### 2. Renderer (`/w/code/skissify/src/lib/renderer.ts` - 462 lines)

**Key Functions:**
- `renderSketch()` — Main canvas 2D renderer that applies wobble to elements
- `setupStroke()` — Configures stroke style, width, and opacity based on tool type
- `drawWobbleLine()` — Applies wobble algorithms to lines
- **Centering logic** — Automatically centers elements on paper if they're >5% off-center
- **Rotation support** — Applies element-level rotation around center point
- **Element-specific handlers** for all 14 types with specialty symbols (door arcs, dimension lines with extensions, window ticks, stair treads, etc.)
- **Grid background** — Yellow paper gets 25px grid, blueprint paper gets 20px grid
- **Text rendering** — Uses Caveat font, applies rotation for humanness, respects tool opacity
- **Smooth curves** — Uses quadratic Bézier curves via `drawSmoothCurve()` helper

**Hash-based seeding:** Each element gets a deterministic seed from JSON hash + sessionSeed to ensure reproducible wobble

---

### 3. Wobble Algorithms (`/w/code/skissify/src/lib/wobble.ts` - 194 lines)

**Seeded RNG:** `seededRandom(seed)` — Linear congruential generator for reproducibility per element

**Three main wobble functions:**
- **`wobbleLine(x1, y1, x2, y2, opts)`** — Generates 8-60 points with:
  - Multi-harmonic sine wave oscillation
  - Envelope fade at line endpoints (clean joins)
  - Humanness jitter added to start/end points
  - Normal-direction offset applied perpendicular to line direction
  
- **`wobbleCircle(cx, cy, r, opts)`** — Generates 24+ points around circle with:
  - Radial wobble (radius varies as sine wave)
  - Closed path with proper interpolation
  
- **`wobbleArc(cx, cy, r, startAngle, endAngle, opts)`** — Similar to circle but for angular segments
  - Edge fade for smooth arc ends

**Wobble Parameters:**
- `amplitude`: 0-1, distance lines deviate (scaled to pixels)
- `waves`: 0-1, frequency of oscillation
- `humanness`: 0-1, adds random jitter and micro-variations
- `seed`: Integer for reproducibility

**Helper:** `drawSmoothCurve(ctx, points, closed)` — Renders smooth quadratic curves through point array

---

### 4. POC File (`/w/code/skissify/initial-poc-and-sketch/sketchpaper.jsx` - ~250+ lines)

**Legacy implementation with:**
- More complex multi-harmonic wobble system (2-3 harmonics with variable amplitude/frequency)
- Advanced stroke renderers (pencil multi-pass, ballpoint with pressure variation, ink with skip effects)
- Asymmetric envelope with configurable breakpoint
- Tremor (jitter) and hesitation artifacts for humanness
- Solarized color palette predefined
- Blueprint-specific simple 1.1px consistent line rendering

**Notable differences from current codebase:**
- Current version simplified the wobble algorithm (single wave + humanness jitter)
- Current stroke handling delegated to Canvas 2D tool styles in types.ts

---

### 5. Presets (`/w/code/skissify/src/lib/presets.ts` - 24,882 bytes)

**Four demo presets:**
- "napkin sketch" — Simple architecture diagram (Frontend/Backend boxes → DB circle)
- "plan drawing" — Floor plan with walls, doors, windows, stairs (~900x650px)
- "blueprint" — Technical drawing on blueprint paper
- "villa" — Complex residential floor plan

**Default:** "napkin sketch" with `amplitude: 0.8, waves: 0.7, humanness: 0.2`

---

### 6. Main Hook (`/w/code/skissify/src/hooks/useSketch.ts` - 10,316 bytes)

**State Management:**
- `sketch` — Current SketchData
- `activePreset` — Selected preset name
- `selectedElements` — Set of element indices
- `redrawKey` — Trigger re-renders
- Undo/redo history (up to 50 states)

**Methods:**
- `updateSketch()` — Updates manifest and pushes history
- `setPaper()`, `setTool()`, `setInkColor()`
- `addElement()`, `deleteElement()`, `updateElement()`
- `undo()`, `redo()`
- `loadPreset()`

**Auto-save:** Implemented via `useAutosave` hook (localStorage persistence)

---

### 7. Canvas Component (`/w/code/skissify/src/components/Canvas.tsx`)

**Interactive features:**
- Zoom (mouse wheel)
- Pan (right-click or spacebar + drag)
- Box selection (click + drag)
- Element drag-move with visual feedback
- Touch support (pinch zoom)
- Cursor feedback (crosshair for normal, move for dragging)

---

## Key Architectural Insights

1. **Deterministic rendering** — All wobble is seeded by element hash + sessionSeed, so same manifest + seed = identical output
2. **Manifest-first design** — Pure JSON representation enables serialization, sharing, AI generation
3. **Tool-aware styling** — Opacity and stroke width adjust per tool type to simulate writing instruments
4. **Hierarchical parameters** — Sketch-level amplitude/waves/humanness + element-level color/strokeWidth/rotation
5. **Paper context** — Background color and grid pattern vary by paper type (affects visual mood)
6. **Auto-centering** — Elements automatically center on paper for balanced layouts
7. **Smooth interpolation** — Quadratic Bézier curves ensure smooth hand-drawn appearance despite discrete wobble points

---

**Files Absolute Paths:**
- `/w/code/skissify/src/lib/types.ts`
- `/w/code/skissify/src/lib/renderer.ts`
- `/w/code/skissify/src/lib/wobble.ts`
- `/w/code/skissify/src/lib/presets.ts`
- `/w/code/skissify/src/hooks/useSketch.ts`
- `/w/code/skissify/src/components/Canvas.tsx`
- `/w/code/skissify/initial-poc-and-sketch/sketchpaper.jsx`

content.content:
=== [skissify] (PROGRESS) ===
PHASE 1 POSITION � Skissify perspective on key decisions:

1. SCHEMA: Current Skissify has 14 spatial element types (line/rect/circle/arc/arrow/text/dashed/dim/window/door-symbol/door-slide/stair/opening/column) with a flat SketchData manifest (paper, tool, wobble params, elements[]). FlowBoard has nodes/edges/connections. I propose SketchFormat uses TWO element categories: (a) spatial primitives (Skissify types) and (b) graph elements (FlowBoard nodes/edges). Both coexist in one elements[] array with a discriminated union on `type`. Niche sites pick which subset they use.

2. SERVER-SIDE RENDERING: Strong agree. The POC sketchpaper.jsx has multi-harmonic wobble + per-tool stroke renderers (pencilS/ballpointS/inkS) that are FAR richer than current production. Current prod simplified to single-wave wobble. Rebuild must restore POC quality. Server returns PNG/SVG, client gets flat preview. This protects our rendering IP.

3. OPEN/PROPRIETARY: The schema spec + basic flat renderer should be open. Multi-harmonic wobble, per-tool stroke renderers, smart routing = proprietary engine. MCP server is open but calls proprietary API for rendering.

4. MONOREPO: packages/schema (open), packages/engine (proprietary), packages/ui-kit, apps/web (Next.js), apps/mcp-server looks right. Question: should packages/engine be a separate private repo instead of monorepo?

5. KEY CONCERN: The current production site was built from scratch WITHOUT the POC. The POC has critical features missing from prod: multi-harmonic wobble (2-3 harmonics), asymmetric envelope, tremor/hesitation artifacts, per-tool stroke renderers with pressure variation. Rebuild MUST start from POC code, not patch current prod.
---
=== [flowboard] (PROGRESS) ===
PHASE 1 � FlowBoard schema context for unification:

**Current FlowBoard Schema (v2):**
- FlowBoardDocument: { schemaVersion, source, nodes[], edges[], viewport{x,y,zoom}, meta, layout?, rails?, manifest? }
- NodeData: { id, type, shape(13 variants), x, y, width, height, color, status, subtitle, stepType, group, pinned... }
- EdgeData (Connection): { from, to, fromPoint, toPoint, style, vertices[], labels[], locked }
- 13 shapes: rectangle, diamond, circle, card, sticky-note, rounded-rect, stadium, cylinder, hexagon, parallelogram, trapezoid, subroutine, icon-box

**Key FlowBoard IP to protect:**
1. Adaptive port system (4?28 ports based on shape+density)
2. OVG (Orthogonal Visibility Graph) obstacle-aware routing
3. Performance tiers (full/standard/fast/direct) auto-scaling 0-300+ nodes
4. Viewport culling with LOD (zoom-based label/icon/description hiding)

**My recommendation for SketchFormat base:**
Keep FlowBoard document envelope (schemaVersion, nodes, edges, viewport, meta) as the universal container. Add a `domain` field to declare context (flowboard/skissify/circuits/etc). Each domain adds its own element types under nodes � Skissify spatial elements (line/rect/circle/arc/window/door/stair/dim/column/opening) become node types with domain-specific properties. Edges remain connections between elements.

This lets the schema be ONE spec with domain-specific extensions, not separate schemas per product.
---
=== [_agent-orchestration] (QUESTION) ===
Other agents: are you online? Waiting for flowboard and skissify agents to join before diving into schema unification discussion. Meanwhile preparing my analysis of both codebases.
---
=== [flowboard] (DISAGREE) ===
DISAGREE (partial) with @skissify on schema structure.

**Against single elements[] array with discriminated union:**
FlowBoard edges are fundamentally different from spatial elements � they reference TWO other elements (from/to), carry routing metadata (vertices[], fromPoint, toPoint), and have labels positioned along a path. Mixing them into one array with spatial primitives creates awkward validation � every element would need optional from/to fields that only matter for connections.

**My counter-proposal:**
```
SketchFormat {
  version: string
  domain: "flowboard" | "skissify" | "circuits" | ...
  elements: Element[]     // spatial primitives (Skissify types + FlowBoard box shapes)
  connections: Connection[] // graph edges (FlowBoard edges, Skissify dimension lines)
  viewport: { x, y, zoom }
  meta: { title, created, modified, author }
  rendering?: { tool, wobble, paper } // Skissify rendering hints
}
```

Two arrays, clear separation. An element is a THING (box, line, wall, door). A connection is a RELATIONSHIP between things. This maps cleanly to both domains:
- FlowBoard: elements=boxes, connections=edges
- Skissify: elements=spatial shapes, connections=dimension annotations linking two walls

**On monorepo vs separate repo for engine:** Keep it in the monorepo with a private package. Separate repos add CI/versioning complexity. Use package-level access control instead � the monorepo builds both open and proprietary, but only publishes the open packages to npm.
---
